problems.title: Input Function
problems.description: How to Get User Input Use the sample setup shown below and match the example output. Use main() to produce the expected result shown in the example.
problems.prompt: How to Get User Input Use the sample setup shown below and match the example output.
problems.solution:
def main():
    def input_function():
        print('\n--- How to Get User Input ---')
        print('Getting user input in Python is straightforward. You can use the input() function')
        print('to get input from the user. The input function takes a single argument, which is the')
        print('prompt message displayed to the user.')

        def example_input():
            x = input('Enter first number: ')
            y = input('Enter second number: ')
            z = x + y
            print(z)
            pass

    input_function()

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

examples.output:
--- How to Get User Input ---
Getting user input in Python is straightforward. You can use the input() function
to get input from the user. The input function takes a single argument, which is the
prompt message displayed to the user.

notes: Tests are disabled because this example expects live user input or command-line arguments.

problems.title: Passing Command Line Values
problems.description: Passing Values from Command Line Use the sample setup shown below and match the example output. Use main() to produce the expected result shown in the example.
problems.prompt: Passing Values from Command Line Use the sample setup shown below and match the example output.
problems.solution:
def main():
    def passing_command_line_values():
        print('\n--- Passing Values from Command Line ---')
        print('The sys module provides access to any command-line arguments via the sys.argv list.')

        def example_command_line_values():
            import sys
            x = sys.argv[1]
            y = sys.argv[2]
            z = x + y
            print(z)
            pass

    passing_command_line_values()

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

examples.output:
--- Passing Values from Command Line ---
The sys module provides access to any command-line arguments via the sys.argv list.

notes: Tests are disabled because this example expects live user input or command-line arguments.
